rules:
Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose 1:
• Put a Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
• Shuffle 3 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Each player switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. Your opponent switches first. (If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, that player doesn't switch Pokémon.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon-GX or Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to gets +40 HP.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Distort
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Sonic Volume
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Special Energy cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Boomburst-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Infinite Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twilight-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle 10 cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Cleave
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Dead End-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chemical Breath
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Hazard-GX
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Peerless Hundred Blows-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the number of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lycanfang-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Bedrock Breaker
attacks.effect: Discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Muscle Punch-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Prismatic Burst
attacks.effect: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Black Ray-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tapu Storm-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Diabolical Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Queen's Haze-GX
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Out-GX
attacks.effect: Discard the top 10 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nature's Judgment
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Tapu Wilderness-GX
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crossing Cut-GX
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Each player counts the cards in their hand, shuffles those cards into their deck, then draws that many cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Put 1 of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all cards attached to it into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, move up to 3 basic Energy cards from that Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Each player counts the cards in their hand, shuffles those cards into their deck, then draws that many cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card. If it's a Supporter card, discard it.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Rotom Dex Poké Finder Mode 122/147 Burning Shadows
rules:
Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back in any order or shuffle them into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Whenever any player plays a Pokémon from their hand to evolve 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon-GX, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless more.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon that has any Water Energy attached to it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Search your deck for up to 4 Fire Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. Your opponent switches first. (If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, that player doesn't switch Pokémon.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to gets +40 HP.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 1 of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all cards attached to it into your hand.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Mix-Up
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

attacks.name: Baby-Doll Eyes
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They live in pitch black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

attacks.name: Bouffant Head
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.effect: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: They charge wildly and headbutt everything. Their headbutts have enough destructive force to derail a train.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Night Raid
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard a Pokémon from it.

flavorText: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

attacks.name: See Through
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: It has a perfect sense of time. Whatever happens, it keeps rhythm by precisely tilting its head in time.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: In order to create a more advanced Pokémon, an additional program was installed, but apparently it contained a defect that makes it move oddly.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: In order to create a more advanced Pokémon, an additional program was installed, but apparently it contained a defect that makes it move oddly.

attacks.name: Calculate
attacks.effect: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: For the purposes of planetary development, Porygon was updated with the most cutting-edge technology available.

attacks.name: Code Check
attacks.effect: Look at 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: Roughly 20 years ago, it was artificially created, utilizing the latest technology of the time.

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.effect: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 60 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has a violent temperament. It will attack anything that looks it in the eye. Its sharp claws inflict deep wounds.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lies around all day, becoming active near dusk. At night, it wanders the city in search of loose change.

attacks.name: Core Enforcer
attacks.effect: Discard a Darkness Energy and a Fairy Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This is Zygarde's form at times when it uses its overwhelming power to suppress those who endanger the ecosystem.

attacks.name: Distort
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Sonic Volume
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Special Energy cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Boomburst-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strength Sap
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.
It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.
It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.
It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it's able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

attacks.name: Sparkling Wish
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Diamond Storm
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Fairy Pokémon.

flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

attacks.name: Infinite Force
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twilight-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle 10 cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.
The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Energy Slosh
attacks.effect: You may move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

attacks.name: Constrict
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

attacks.name: Dark Cleave
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Dead End-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Cleave
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Dead End-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dark Raid
attacks.effect: If your opponent has already used their GX attack, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

attacks.name: Rule of Evil
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to each Pokémon that has an Ability (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slash attack's base damage is 80.

flavorText: It has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.

attacks.name: Chemical Breath
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Hazard-GX
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Division
attacks.effect: Search your deck for Alolan Grimer and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Slippery Sludge
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Grimer, which had been brought in to solve a problem with garbage, developed over time into this form.

attacks.name: Enhanced Fang
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It forms a group of Rattata, which it assumes command of. Each group has its own territory, and disputes over food happen often.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Bite attack's base damage is 60.

flavorText: With its incisors, it gnaws through doors and infiltrates people's homes. Then, with a twitch of its whiskers, it steals whatever food it finds.

attacks.name: Peerless Hundred Blows-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Intentional Grounding
attacks.effect: Discard a Pokémon Tool card from your hand. If you don't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

attacks.name: Kick Away
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Vigorous Dash
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The mud stuck to Mudbray's hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.
Its quick movements confuse its enemies. Well equipped with claws and fangs, it also uses the sharp rocks in its mane as weapons.

attacks.name: Dangerous Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: The more intimidating the opponent it faces, the more this Pokémon's blood boils. It will attack with no regard for its own safety.

attacks.name: Gutsy Hammer
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It aimed for the top but got lost and ended up on a snowy mountain. Being forced to endure the cold, this Pokémon evolved and grew fur.
While guarding its weak points with its pincers, it looks for an opening and unleashes punches. When it loses, it foams at the mouth and faints.

attacks.name: Quick Guard
attacks.effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. This Pokémon can't use Quick Guard during your next turn.

attacks.name: Brick Break
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance or any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Desiring the strongest karate chop, they seclude themselves in mountains and train without sleeping.
Not only does it perceive auras, but it has also gained the power to control them. It employs them in battle.
It can discern the physical and emotional states of people, Pokémon, and other natural things from the shape of their aura waves.

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Solar Heat
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.
Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

attacks.name: Rock Wrecker
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: From holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Megahorn
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Bedrock Breaker
attacks.effect: Discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Muscle Punch-GX
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Prismatic Burst
attacks.effect: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Black Ray-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dust Storm
attacks.effect: Discard all cards from both Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Born from a sand mound playfully built by a child, this Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Hand Kinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck to dust.

attacks.name: Perplexing Eyes
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now Psychic until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

flavorText: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Steamroller
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison.

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Boost
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove that Special Condition from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

attacks.name: Night Roam
attacks.effect: Put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

attacks.name: Dark Guidance
attacks.effect: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

attacks.name: Shadowy Knot
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

attacks.name: Drift Ashore
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Unarmed
attacks.effect: If you have no cards in your hand, ignore all Energy in this attack's cost.

flavorText: A poisonous bite reacted with its system, blessing it with the enhanced intellect of a genius. It has full control of its psychic powers.

attacks.name: Electrosmash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers.

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Vacuum Bolt
attacks.effect: You may do 80 more damage. If you do, this attack does 80 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

attacks.name: Small Appetite
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon's maximum HP is 100 or more, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

attacks.name: Aqua Shock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

attacks.name: Thunder Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It pushes the tips of its tails against its foes and then lets loose a high-voltage current. Its foes are burned to a crisp in an instant.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Half of all sudden blackouts are caused by Electabuzz gathering at electric power plants and gobbling up electricity.

attacks.name: Volt Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It unleashes electric shocks that can reach 100,000 volts. When agitated, it can knock out even an Indian elephant.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tapu Storm-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.effect: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tapu Storm-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gnash Teeth
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Synchronoise
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that shares a type with your opponent's Active Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it unleashes its psychic power from the protuberance on its head, the grating sound of grinding teeth echoes through the area.

attacks.name: Aqua Reflect
attacks.effect: Move a Water Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The high-pressure water expelled from its tail is so powerful, it can destroy a concrete wall.
The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. It waters plants with it using its tail.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of living things on riverbeds.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The fur on its body naturally repels water. It can stay dry, even when it plays in the water.

attacks.name: Venting Anger
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Magikarp in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Splash Burn
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A tale is told of a town that angered Gyarados. Before the sun rose the next day, flames utterly consumed the town, leaving not a trace behind.

attacks.name: Splashing Dodge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Although weak and helpless, this Pokémon is incredibly fertile. They exist in such multitudes, you'll soon grow tired of seeing them.

attacks.name: Brine
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tornado Shot
attacks.effect: Discard a Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body bristles with sharp spikes. Carelessly trying to touch it could cause fainting from the spikes.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Known to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.
It creates drops of ice in its coat and showers them over its enemies. Anyone who angers it will be frozen stiff in an instant.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

attacks.name: Flame Cloak
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The shell on its back is chemically unstable and explodes violently if struck. The hole in its stomach is its weak point.

attacks.name: Diabolical Claws
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Queen's Haze-GX
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Using their very hot, flame-covered tongues, they burn through Durant's steel bodies and consume their insides.

attacks.name: Flare Recharge
attacks.effect: Attach a Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.
Very intelligent, it roasts berries before eating them. It likes to help people.

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Phoenix Burn
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Phoenix Burn during your next turn.

attacks.name: Eternal Flame-GX
attacks.effect: Put 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon-GX or Fire Pokémon-EX from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Out-GX
attacks.effect: Discard the top 10 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.
The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.
The flame on its tail indicates Charmander's life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crossing Cut-GX
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

attacks.name: Bubble Net
attacks.effect: Energy can't be attached to the Defending Pokémon from your opponent's hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

attacks.name: Leaf Supply
attacks.effect: You may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.
It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

attacks.name: Pitch
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies' bellies, then lifts and throws them.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: While it's believed that starlight provides it with energy, this Pokémon also loves to eat berries. In the daytime, it curls up in the grass to sleep.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They are timid and grow uneasy when not in a swarm with others of their kind. The pattern on their backs differs slightly from one to another.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crosswise Whip
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

attacks.name: Downer Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

attacks.name: Stinky Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Smells incredibly foul! However, around one out of a thousand people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

attacks.name: Bye-Bye Heal
attacks.effect: Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Close examination of its large eyes reveals that each eye is composed of a myriad of tiny eyes.

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.effect: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its shell is filled with its soft innards. It doesn't move much because of the risk it might carelessly spill its innards out.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When attacked by bird Pokémon, it resists by releasing a terrifically strong odor from its antennae, but it often becomes their prey.
